advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Distinguish a permanent signing failure (missing/broken key, refuse-guard) from a transient one before mapping to exit 11, so a non-retryable sign error does not loop.
evidence: src/rebar/_cli/_llm_commands.py:119: `if sig.get("signed") is False and sig.get("error"):` then `return 11`
src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py:499: `verdict["signature"] = {"signed": False, "error": str(exc)}` — set for ANY Exception from sign_plan_review, not only transient lock errors
src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py:495 comment: 'a missing key is a real signal' — i.e. a permanent, operator-actionable condition takes the same error path
src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py:303-308: `raise SigningError("refusing to sign a non-PASS / degraded plan-review verdict" ...)` — a structural (permanent) failure also becomes signature.error
ABSENCE: no classification of the error string / exception type to separate retryable (lock) from permanent (missing key, refuse-guard); every error → exit 11
advisory.finding: A PASS whose signing failed for a PERMANENT reason (missing/broken signing key, or the refuse-to-sign guard) is mapped to exit 11 'transient — retry', causing an unbounded re-run loop that fails identically each time. `sign_plan_review` raises `SigningError` for both transient (index.lock) AND permanent conditions, all recorded as the same `{signed:False, error:...}` shape, which the new branch cannot distinguish.
scenarios: Operator runs review-plan with no/broken signing key: verdict PASS, sign raises, exit 11; a harness that auto-retries 11 re-runs the full ~10min review and fails the same way indefinitely.
advisory.suggested_fix: Classify the SigningError before returning 11 (e.g. propagate a `retryable` flag or resolution_class from sign_plan_review into signature, and only return 11 when it indicates a transient/lock condition; otherwise return 1).

advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Update docs/exit-codes.md, ADR 0040, and the test docstring to record the signing-failure trigger for exit 11 (or choose a distinct code), keeping the frozen contract accurate.
evidence: docs/exit-codes.md:20: exit 11 = 'degraded on a systemic, retryable LLM failure (rate-limit / connection blip — the classifier's WAIT_AND_RETRY / RETRY_NOW disposition)'
docs/adr/0040-exit-11-block-but-retryable.md:31-33: '...for the systemic, retryable subset of LLM-gate degrades, i.e. the classifier's WAIT_AND_RETRY / RETRY_NOW disposition (retryable == True)'
docs/exit-codes.md:9-10: 'This contract is frozen ... Changes to an emitted code are contract changes and must be called out in release notes.'
tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_exit_codes.py:241: `assert "`11`" in text and "block-but-retryable" in text.lower()` — only greps a literal, so a new emitter of 11 is not enforced to be documented
advisory.finding: Exit 11's contract — frozen in docs/exit-codes.md, ADR 0040, and the test module docstring — is defined strictly as the LLM-failure classifier's WAIT_AND_RETRY/RETRY_NOW disposition (coverage.retryable). This change introduces a NEW trigger for exit 11 (a PASS whose attestation write failed) without updating that documented/frozen contract, and the guard test only checks the literal string '11', so the drift is not caught.
scenarios: A future reader/driver consults exit-codes.md, sees 11 == 'model provider hiccup', and does not realize a signing-persistence failure now also emits 11 — the doc no longer describes the full set of emitters.

advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a capsys/capfd assertion that the failed-sign PASS path writes the actionable stderr message and interpolates the sign error, so the AC's 'prints the sign error prominently' behavior is regression-protected (not just the exit code).
evidence: Source emits observable stderr: src/rebar/_cli/_llm_commands.py:120-124 `sys.stderr.write("plan review PASSED but the attestation could not be persisted: " f"{sig.get('error')}\n" ...)`
test asserts only return value: test_exit_codes.py:201 `assert _disposition_exit_code(sign_failed, indeterminate_code=2) == 11`
ABSENCE: `search_files` for 'stderr' in test_exit_codes.py returns no matches — no capsys/capfd assertion on the emitted message anywhere in the file
Ticket AC (5147-fa08-4953-4de5): 'the CLI exits NON-ZERO and prints the sign error prominently (no silent exit-0/PASS ...)' — the message prominence is an explicit acceptance criterion
advisory.finding: The new tests assert only the exit code (11) for the failed-sign PASS but never assert the actionable stderr message the diff emits (the 'plan review PASSED but the attestation could not be persisted: {error} ... re-run rebar review-plan' text with the sign error interpolated). The ticket AC requires the CLI 'prints the sign error prominently' — the loud signal is a first-class deliverable — yet a regression that dropped or mangled that message, or failed to surface the error, would leave both new tests green.
scenarios: A future edit drops the stderr write or stops interpolating sig['error']; exit 11 still returned, tests stay green, but the operator loses the actionable 're-run' signal the change exists to provide.
advisory.suggested_fix: In test_signable_pass_that_failed_to_sign_exits_11_not_0, accept `capsys` and after the call assert the captured stderr contains the interpolated error substring and the 're-run `rebar review-plan`' guidance.
